Fix: ReAct agent aggressive prompt + custom_payload injection + multi-class detection

- Passive prompt (Inject=0): rewrote prompt to default INJECT for all
  non-static endpoints; added 12 new _INTERESTING_RX patterns covering
  .php/.asp/.jsp, /vulnerabilities/, file/include/search params, etc.

- No custom_payload: every INJECT now requires param/value/method/detect_by;
  payload rules: numeric->SQLi SLEEP, file->LFI traversal, search->XSS
  polyglot, login->SQLi auth bypass, admin->SSTI template injection

- Single-dimension detection: new _probe_with_payload() with 5 strategies:
  time_delay (blind SQLi), error_marker (SQLi/LFI/SSTI text), reflection
  (XSS/SSTI), status_change (403->200, 500 triggered), size_diff (IDOR)

- MAX_ENDPOINTS 15->20, MAX_PROBES 30->50 (env-overridable)
- _run_probes() dispatches custom_payload first, IDOR mutations as fallback

commit.message:
Fix: clone Response for deduped requests to prevent body-consumed error (#32)

The request deduplication returned the same Promise<Response> to
multiple callers. Response.json() can only be called once per
Response object, so the second caller got 'Body has already been
consumed'. Now deduped callers get response.clone() so each can
independently consume the body.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: update cockpit-inferno submodule (PTP state/grandmaster fix)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ptp): correct state detection and grandmaster parsing for statime TRACE logs

State: old code checked for 'locked'/'synchroniz'/'acquir' keywords which
don't appear in TRACE-level logs. New code parses the DEBUG
'Recommended state port N: Some(S1(...))' line — S1/M1/M2/M3 = locked,
None = acquiring.

Grandmaster: old regex expected 'grandmaster: XX:XX:XX' format. Actual
format is 'clock_uuid: [16, 231, 198, 17, 15, 4]' (decimal bytes in
GrandmasterPropertiesV1). New code parses bytes and formats as hex MAC.

Journal window: -n 80 → --since=-60s piped through grep for only the
relevant lines (Estimated offset + Recommended state port), keeping
output small while covering a full minute.

Stability fallback: if no Recommended state line found, infer locked
from offset stability (all last 5 offsets < 1ms).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: install lifecycle callbacks before invoke to prevent race condition

The lifecycle callbacks (onConnectionComplete/onConnectionFailed) were
installed AFTER api.resumeSession() returned, inside a .andThen() block.
When Rust found an existing session client (fast path), the tokio::spawn
task could complete and emit the SSE lifecycle event before the frontend
installed the callbacks — causing the event to be silently dropped and
the 90s watchdog to fire.

Fix: install callbacks synchronously before firing the invoke, ensuring
they are ready before Rust can possibly emit the lifecycle event.

Also adds a panic guard in Rust: a follow-up task awaits the JoinHandle
and emits ConnectionFailed if the main task panics, preventing silent
watchdog timeouts.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

refactor: fire-and-forget session resume with lifecycle events

Replace the blocking acp_resume_session invoke (19-21s) with a
fire-and-forget pattern. The Rust command now validates synchronously
and spawns the heavy work via tokio::spawn, returning immediately.

Connection completion/failure is signaled through ConnectionComplete
and ConnectionFailed SessionUpdate variants delivered via the existing
SSE bridge. The frontend connectSession waits for these lifecycle
events instead of blocking on the invoke.

Key changes:
- Rust: acp_resume_session accepts attempt_id, returns Result<(), Error>
  after validation, spawns async task with 45s encompassing timeout
- Rust: Added publish_direct() on AcpUiEvent for lifecycle events
  that bypass rate-limited dispatch
- TS: SessionEventService handles connectionComplete/connectionFailed
  with attempt_id for stale event detection
- TS: connectSession uses event callbacks + 90s watchdog instead of
  withTimeout wrapper around blocking invoke
- Tests: Updated 41 connection manager tests for lifecycle event pattern

自动备份清理逻辑原本使用 `Directory.EnumerateDirectories` 查找旧备份，但自动备份实际均为 `.zip` 文件，导致查询结果始终为空，`AutoBackupLimit` 限制未生效，备份文件会无限累积。这个提交将 `Directory.EnumerateDirectories` 改为 `Directory.EnumerateFiles`、`Directory.Delete` 改为 `File.Delete`，以正确识别并清理过期备份文件，使备份数量限制正常生效。

core/ai.py — AIClient.complete() robustness:

    \x0e-\x1f, \x7f) that cause 400 Bad Request on OpenRouter;
    truncates to 12000 chars (safely under model context limits)
  - Parse JSON errors from API 400 responses and log the actual
    error.message for debugging (was silently returning code only)
  - Guard choices[0] key access with try/except + log on bad shape
  - Import re (was missing)

recon/js_hunter.py — NOISE_BLACKLIST + _CDN_URL_DOMAINS expansion:
  - Added Web3/blockchain (infura.io, alchemy.com, moralis.io,
    quicknode.com, ankr.com, ipfs.io, pinata.cloud, etherscan.io,
    polygonscan.com, ethereum.org, openzeppelin.com)

    honeycomb.io, lightstep.com, opentelemetry.io)

  - Added fintech SDKs (plaid.com, braintreepayments.com, adyen.com)
  - _CDN_URL_DOMAINS regex synced with the new noise list

core/filter.py — Filter 3: SSRF-without-OOB:
  - Real SSRF leaves AWS/GCP metadata content in extracted-results
    (ami-XXXXX, instance-id, local-hostname, instanceId, privateIp…)
  - Reflection FPs have the injected URL in the response body but no
    metadata content — this was the source of all 29 SSRF FPs in the
    previous scan
  - New filter: if template-id matches 'ssrf' AND no OOB interaction
    marker AND no metadata evidence in extracted-results → drop as
    'SSRF-NoOOB'
  - Renumbered downstream filters (3→4 WAF, 4→5 HTML, 5→6 PH…)
